participants.reasoning: The extracted description matches the official participant characteristics in key details, including the randomized sample size, age range, gender mix, severe pulmonary hypertension, low LVEF, and elective valve surgery population.
participants.extracted: Twenty adult patients undergoing cardiac valve surgery were enrolled between May 2006 and July 2007 and randomized to two equal groups: 10 patients received levosimendan and 10 served as controls. All patients had severe pulmonary arterial hypertension (systolic pulmonary artery pressure ≥ 60 mmHg) and low left ventricular ejection fraction (< 50%). The sample was middle-aged on average (mean age about 46–50 years), with both men and women included. Most were NYHA class III, and preoperative systolic pulmonary artery pressure was markedly elevated (around 71–73 mmHg). The surgical population included mitral valve replacement, aortic and mitral valve replacement, mitral repair, and some redo procedures; a small number also underwent concomitant CABG.
participants.official:
N randomised:20
N lost to follow‐up or withdrawn:0
N analysed:20
Mean age (years):intervention group: 49.6 ± 10.7; comparison group: 45.7 ± 7.9
Gender (male/female):intervention group: 5/5; comparison group: 3/7
Participant with or without pre‐existing LV dysfunction:with
Type of cardiac surgery:elective valve surgery
Inclusion criteria:severe pulmonary arterial hypertension (systolic pulmonary artery pressure ≥ 60 mmHg); LVEF ≤ 50%
Exclusion criteria:no information given

interventions.reasoning: The extracted description matches the official intervention dosing and timing for levosimendan and is compatible with standard care in the control group.
interventions.extracted: Levosimendan was administered to the intervention group after anesthetic induction, using a 12 μg/kg loading dose over 10 minutes followed by a continuous 24-hour infusion at 0.1 μg/kg/min. The control group received no levosimendan and was managed with standard perioperative care under the same anesthetic and surgical protocol.
interventions.official:
Intervention:levosimendan; loading dose of 12 µg/kg within 10 min started with the induction of anaesthesia followed by 0.1 µg/kg/min for 24 hours
Comparison:standard cardiac care
Concomitant medication:inotropic drugs
Excluded medications:no information given

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes match the official primary haemodynamic measures and secondary clinical safety outcomes, with broadly corresponding postoperative time points.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure hemodynamic outcomes including cardiac output (CO), cardiac index (CI), systemic vascular resistance (SVR), pulmonary vascular resistance (PVR), and mean pulmonary artery pressure (MPAP). These were assessed using thermodilution catheter monitoring at baseline after insertion of the catheter and then repeatedly over time: after the levosimendan loading dose, at 6 hours, 12 hours, and 24 hours of levosimendan infusion, with equivalent time points in the control group. The study also recorded perioperative and clinical outcomes including need for inotropic drugs, need for intra-aortic balloon pump (IABP), mortality, postoperative exploration, low cardiac output, acute renal failure, length of ICU stay, and length of hospital stay, measured during the postoperative hospitalization period.
outcomes.official:
Primary:haemodynamics
Secondary:safety data (incidence of LCOS/mortality, need for IABP/inotropic support, length of ICU stay/hospital stay)
Time points reported:preoperative; after induction of anaesthesia; after administration of the loading dose; 6/12/24 hours after start of medication; on discharge from hospital
